headline: Cashless Payments Reach 68% of Malaysian Transactions
context: Bank Negara reported 68% of retail transactions were cashless in 2025 up from 42% in 2020 led by DuitNow QR.

cards.text: Cashless payments reach 68% of retail transactions in 2025
cards.sub: DuitNow QR processed 1.2 billion transactions worth RM85 billion

cards.h: e-Wallet users grew to 28 million with Touch n Go and GrabPay at 62% share
cards.s: Average Malaysian makes 4.2 cashless transactions daily up from 1.8 in 2020
cards.d: BNM data on e-wallet user count and transaction frequency. The Malaysian payments landscape has shifted dramatically since the pandemic, with cashless now dominant in retail.

cards.h: Rural cashless adoption lags at 35% versus 78% in urban areas
cards.s: Limited merchant coverage and internet reliability constrain rural uptake
cards.d: The urban-rural divide in cashless adoption is wide. Connectivity gaps, merchant onboarding rates, and digital literacy all contribute. East Malaysia and rural Peninsular lag significantly.

cards.h: Bank Negara identified RM2.8 billion in e-wallet fraud up 85% from prior year
cards.s: Unauthorized transactions and social engineering are fastest growing categories
cards.d: BNM tracks payment fraud through its Financial Stability Report and quarterly data. The fraud growth rate substantially outpaces the growth in transaction volume, indicating worsening per-transaction risk.

cards.h: Cashless growth tests whether financial inclusion and fraud prevention scale together
cards.text: Headline adoption numbers obscure the question of whether protection scales as fast as participation. If 68% are cashless but fraud grows 85% year-on-year, the system is moving in two directions at once — more users, more risk per user.

68% cashless transactions. Financial inclusion requires that the 32% using cash are not left behind. The remaining cash users are disproportionately elderly, rural, and B40. Policy that pushes harder on cashless without protecting cash acceptance at essential services risks excluding the most vulnerable from healthcare, government services, and basic commerce.

sources: BNM Annual Report 2025, BNM Payment Systems Report, DuitNow / PayNet data, Financial Stability Report
confidence: Medium — The 68% retail cashless figure, DuitNow QR 1.2 billion transactions worth RM85 billion, 28 million e-wallet users, TNG/GrabPay 62% combined share, RM2.8 billion e-wallet fraud, 85% YoY fraud growth, and 35%/78% rural-urban split all need primary-source verification before publication. This is a draft requiring fact-check.
